notes.introduction: An exposition of Genesis 48:1-22 on Jacob's blessing of Joseph's two sons, Ephraim and Manasseh, read alongside Hebrews 11:21 as a record of dying worship. The passage is treated in three movements: Jacob's praise for God's past provision, his present rejoicing at seeing Joseph and his sons, and his prophetic blessing placing Ephraim above the firstborn Manasseh.
themes: passing faith to children and grandchildren

sections.heading: Jacob's Praise for God's Past Blessings
The Present: Adoption of Ephraim and Manasseh
The Prophecy: Crossing of Hands and Ephraim's Blessing
Numbers 1 and 2: Fulfillment of the Prophecy
Worship in Scripture: 2 Chronicles 29, Malachi 1, Revelation 4 and 5
Application: Worshiping God Past, Present, and Future
